---
title: "Oracle Summary"
author: "Huiding Chen"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    code_folding: "hide"
---

```{r,include=FALSE}
library(knitr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(cat)
library(survey)
library(cowplot)
library(kableExtra)
library(VGAM)
library(distributionsrd)
library(ggplot2)
library(truncnorm)
library(reshape2)
library(paletteer)
library(plotly)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)
library(RVAideMemoire)
library(glmnet)
library(DT)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE,message = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# Load i=1 separately (different filename)
filenames <- c( paste0("S3_full_summary_", 1:6))

out_mean_list <- lapply(filenames, function(nm) {
  out <- readRDS(paste0(nm, ".RData"))
  out$out_mean
})

names(out_mean_list) <- filenames
```

```{r}
methods_keep <- c( "trueweight", "ps_vs_stepwise_twoway_rake", "svy_oneway_rake",
                   "oracle_rails")

out_mean_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(out_mean_list), function(i) {
  df <- out_mean_list[[i]]
  df$method <- rownames(df)
  df$scenario <- i
  df[df$method %in% methods_keep, ]
}))

out_mean_df <- out_mean_df %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method, 
                         "ps_vs_stepwise_twoway_rake" = "RAILS",
                         "trueweight" = "Trueweight",
                         "svy_oneway_rake" = "Cal_1",
                         "oracle_rails" = "True_RAILS")) %>%
  rename(`Nominal Coverage` = TrueCoverage)

rownames(out_mean_df) <- NULL

out_mean_df <- out_mean_df %>% 
  select(method, scenario, everything())

size_labels <- sapply(filenames, function(nm) {
  out <- readRDS(paste0(nm, ".RData"))
  sum(out$out_size)
})
size_labels_fmt <- paste0(round(size_labels / 1000), "k")
size_labels_fmt <- rev(size_labels_fmt)
```



```{r}
# Extract rails_varcor coverage for each scenario
varcor_rows <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(filenames), function(i) {
  out <- readRDS(paste0(filenames[i], ".RData"))
  data.frame(
    method = "RAILS_Stacked",
    scenario = i,
    `Nominal Coverage` = out$out_varcor["cover_full_temp"],
    `Oracle Coverage` = out$out_varcor_cov[3],
    check.names = FALSE
  )
}))

varcor_rows_2 <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(filenames), function(i) {
  out <- readRDS(paste0(filenames[i], ".RData"))
  data.frame(
    method = "Cal_1_Stacked",
    scenario = i,
    `Nominal Coverage` = out$out_varcor_svy["cover_full_temp"],
    `Oracle Coverage` = out$out_varcor_cov[2],
    check.names = FALSE
  )
}))

varcor_rows_3 <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(filenames), function(i) {
  out <- readRDS(paste0(filenames[i], ".RData"))
  data.frame(
    method = "True_RAILS_Stacked",
    scenario = i,
    `Nominal Coverage` = out$out_varcor_oracle["cover_full_temp"],
    `Oracle Coverage` = out$out_varcor_cov[4],
    check.names = FALSE
  )
}))

varcor_rows_4 <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(filenames), function(i) {
  out <- readRDS(paste0(filenames[i], ".RData"))
  data.frame(
    method             = "Cal_1_Survey",
    scenario           = i,
    `Nominal Coverage` = out$out_varcor_svy["cover_naive_temp"],
    `Oracle Coverage`  = out$out_varcor_cov["svy_one_rake_survey"],
    check.names        = FALSE
  )
}))

# Method display order for legend
p1_method_levels <- c(
  "RAILS", "RAILS_Stacked",
  "True_RAILS", "True_RAILS_Stacked",
  "Cal_1", "Cal_1_Stacked",
  "Trueweight"
)

# Color: same method family shares a color
# RAILS family = red, True_RAILS family = blue, Cal_1 family = green, Trueweight = purple
p1_color <- c(
  "RAILS"              = "#E41A1C",
  "RAILS_Stacked"      = "#E41A1C",
  "True_RAILS"         = "#377EB8",
  "True_RAILS_Stacked" = "#377EB8",
  "Cal_1"              = "#4DAF4A",
  "Cal_1_Stacked"      = "#4DAF4A",
  "Trueweight"         = "#984EA3"
)

# Linetype & shape: same estimator type shares the same style
# Naive/simple = solid + circle, Stacked = dashed + triangle
p1_lty <- c(
  "RAILS"              = "solid",
  "RAILS_Stacked"      = "dashed",
  "True_RAILS"         = "solid",
  "True_RAILS_Stacked" = "dashed",
  "Cal_1"              = "solid",
  "Cal_1_Stacked"      = "dashed",
  "Trueweight"         = "solid"
)

p1_shape <- c(
  "RAILS"              = 16,
  "RAILS_Stacked"      = 17,
  "True_RAILS"         = 16,
  "True_RAILS_Stacked" = 17,
  "Cal_1"              = 16,
  "Cal_1_Stacked"      = 17,
  "Trueweight"         = 16
)

p1_df <- bind_rows(out_mean_df, varcor_rows, varcor_rows_2, varcor_rows_3) %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method,
                         "ps_vs_stepwise_twoway_rake" = "rails"),
         method   = factor(method, levels = p1_method_levels),
         scenario = factor(scenario, levels = 6:1)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(`Nominal Coverage`, `Oracle Coverage`),
               names_to  = "coverage_type",
               values_to = "coverage")

p1 <- ggplot(p1_df,
             aes(x        = scenario,
                 y        = coverage,
                 color    = method,
                 linetype = method,
                 shape    = method,
                 group    = method)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ coverage_type) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = size_labels_fmt) +
  scale_color_manual(values = p1_color,    name = "Method",
                     breaks = p1_method_levels, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = p1_lty,   name = "Method",
                        breaks = p1_method_levels, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = p1_shape,    name = "Method",
                     breaks = p1_method_levels, drop = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.95, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  labs(x = "Sample Size", y = "Coverage") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.key.width = unit(1, "cm")
  ) +
  guides(
    color    = guide_legend(order = 1, nrow = 2, byrow = FALSE,
                            override.aes = list(linewidth = 1)),
    linetype = guide_legend(order = 1, nrow = 2, byrow = FALSE),
    shape    = guide_legend(order = 1, nrow = 2, byrow = FALSE)
  )

ggplotly(p1)
```

```{r}
p2 <- out_mean_df %>%
  filter(method %in% c("True_RAILS", "RAILS")) %>%
  mutate(scenario = factor(scenario, levels = 6:1)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(`Nominal Coverage`, SvyCov),
               names_to = "coverage_type",
               values_to = "coverage") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = scenario, y = coverage, color = method, group = method)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~ coverage_type) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = size_labels_fmt) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.95, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  labs(x = "Sample Size", y = "Coverage", color = "Method") +
  theme_bw()

ggplotly(p2)
```

```{r}
# Extract CI data from out_mean_list
ci_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(out_mean_list), function(i) {
  df <- out_mean_list[[i]]
  df$method <- rownames(df)
  df$scenario <- i
  df[df$method %in% methods_keep, c("method", "scenario", "mean", "Emp SD")]
})) %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method, 
                         "ps_vs_stepwise_twoway_rake" = "RAILS",
                         "trueweight" = "Trueweight",
                         "svy_oneway_rake" = "Cal_1",
                         "oracle_rails" = "True_RAILS"),
         ci_lower = mean - 1.96 * `Emp SD`,
         ci_upper = mean + 1.96 * `Emp SD`)

# Extract true value from trueweight (constant across scenarios)
true_value <- ci_df %>% 
  filter(method == "Trueweight") %>% 
  pull(mean) %>% 
  mean()

varcor_ci_rows <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(filenames), function(i) {
  out <- readRDS(paste0(filenames[i], ".RData"))
  data.frame(
    method = "RAILS_Varcor",
    scenario = i,
    mean = out$out_varcor["mu_hat"],
    `Emp SD` = out$out_varcor_empsd,
    check.names = FALSE
  )
})) %>%
  mutate(ci_lower = mean - 1.96 * `Emp SD`,
         ci_upper = mean + 1.96 * `Emp SD`)

# Bind with existing ci_df and plot
p3 <- bind_rows(ci_df, varcor_ci_rows) %>%
  mutate(scenario = factor(scenario, levels = 6:1)) %>%
  filter(method != "Trueweight") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = scenario, y = mean, group = method, color = method)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), width = 0.3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = true_value, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~ method) +
  labs(x = "Sample Size", y = "Mean", color = "Method",
       caption = "Dashed line = true value (trueweight)",
       title = "Confidence interval with Empirical SD") +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = size_labels_fmt) 
ggplotly(p3)
```
```{r}
# Extract CI data from out_mean_list
ci_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(out_mean_list), function(i) {
  df <- out_mean_list[[i]]
  df$method <- rownames(df)
  df$scenario <- i
  df[df$method %in% methods_keep, c("method", "scenario", "mean", "Avg SD")]
})) %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method, 
                         "ps_vs_stepwise_twoway_rake" = "RAILS",
                         "trueweight" = "Trueweight",
                         "oracle_rails" = "True_RAILS",
                         "svy_oneway_rake" = "Cal_1") ,
         ci_lower = mean - 1.96 * `Avg SD`,
         ci_upper = mean + 1.96 * `Avg SD`)

# Extract true value from trueweight (constant across scenarios)
true_value <- ci_df %>% 
  filter(method == "Trueweight") %>% 
  pull(mean) %>% 
  mean()

varcor_ci_rows <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(filenames), function(i) {
  out <- readRDS(paste0(filenames[i], ".RData"))
  data.frame(
    method = "RAILS_Varcor",
    scenario = i,
    mean = out$out_varcor["mu_hat"],
    `Avg SD` = out$out_varcor["se_full"],
    check.names = FALSE
  )
})) %>%
  mutate(ci_lower = mean - 1.96 * `Avg SD`,
         ci_upper = mean + 1.96 * `Avg SD`)

# Bind with existing ci_df and plot
p4 <- bind_rows(ci_df, varcor_ci_rows) %>%
  filter(method != "Trueweight") %>%
  mutate(scenario = factor(scenario, levels = 6:1)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = scenario, y = mean, group = method, color = method)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), width = 0.3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = true_value, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~ method) +
  labs(x = "Sample Size", y = "Mean", color = "Method",
       caption = "Dashed line = true value (trueweight)",
       title = "Confidence interval with Mean of Estimated SD") +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = size_labels_fmt) 
ggplotly(p4)
```

```{r}
p1
p2
p3
p4
ggsave("Nominal-Oracle Coverage.png", plot = p1, dpi = 1200, width = 8, height = 5, units = "in")
ggsave("CI Emp SD.png", plot = p3, dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")
ggsave("CI Avg SD.png", plot = p4, dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")
```


```{r fig.width=12, fig.height=10}
# ── Build CI data for each method and SD type ──

# --- Original RAILS: Empirical, Simplified, Stacked ---
ci_orig <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(out_mean_list), function(i) {
  df <- out_mean_list[[i]]
  df$method <- rownames(df)
  df$scenario <- i
  df[df$method == "ps_vs_stepwise_twoway_rake",
     c("method", "scenario", "mean", "Emp SD", "Avg SD")]
})) %>%
  mutate(method = "Original_RAILS") %>%
  left_join(
    do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(filenames), function(i) {
      out <- readRDS(paste0(filenames[i], ".RData"))
      data.frame(scenario = i, se_stack = out$out_varcor["se_full"], check.names = FALSE)
    })),
    by = "scenario"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    emp_lower   = mean - 1.96 * `Emp SD`,  emp_upper   = mean + 1.96 * `Emp SD`,
    naive_lower = mean - 1.96 * `Avg SD`,  naive_upper = mean + 1.96 * `Avg SD`,
    stack_lower = mean - 1.96 * se_stack,  stack_upper = mean + 1.96 * se_stack
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(emp_lower, emp_upper, naive_lower, naive_upper, stack_lower, stack_upper),
    names_to = c("ci_type", ".value"), names_pattern = "(.*)_(lower|upper)"
  ) %>%
  mutate(ci_type = recode(ci_type,
                          "emp" = "Empirical SD", "naive" = "Simplified SD", "stack" = "Stacked SD"))

# --- Oracle RAILS: Empirical, Simplified, Stacked ---
ci_oracle <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(out_mean_list), function(i) {
  df <- out_mean_list[[i]]
  df$method <- rownames(df)
  df$scenario <- i
  df[df$method == "oracle_rails",
     c("method", "scenario", "mean", "Emp SD", "Avg SD")]
})) %>%
  mutate(method = "Oracle_RAILS") %>%
  left_join(
    do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(filenames), function(i) {
      out <- readRDS(paste0(filenames[i], ".RData"))
      data.frame(scenario = i, se_stack = out$out_varcor_oracle["se_full"], check.names = FALSE)
    })),
    by = "scenario"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    emp_lower   = mean - 1.96 * `Emp SD`,  emp_upper   = mean + 1.96 * `Emp SD`,
    naive_lower = mean - 1.96 * `Avg SD`,  naive_upper = mean + 1.96 * `Avg SD`,
    stack_lower = mean - 1.96 * se_stack,  stack_upper = mean + 1.96 * se_stack
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(emp_lower, emp_upper, naive_lower, naive_upper, stack_lower, stack_upper),
    names_to = c("ci_type", ".value"), names_pattern = "(.*)_(lower|upper)"
  ) %>%
  mutate(ci_type = recode(ci_type,
                          "emp" = "Empirical SD", "naive" = "Simplified SD", "stack" = "Stacked SD"))

# --- Cal_1: Empirical, Simplified, Stacked (Survey SD removed) ---
ci_svy <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(out_mean_list), function(i) {
  df <- out_mean_list[[i]]
  df$method <- rownames(df)
  df$scenario <- i
  df[df$method == "svy_oneway_rake",
     c("method", "scenario", "mean", "Emp SD", "Avg SD")]
})) %>%
  mutate(method = "Cal_1") %>%
  left_join(
    do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(filenames), function(i) {
      out <- readRDS(paste0(filenames[i], ".RData"))
      data.frame(scenario = i, se_stack = out$out_varcor_svy["se_full"], check.names = FALSE)
    })),
    by = "scenario"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    emp_lower   = mean - 1.96 * `Emp SD`,  emp_upper   = mean + 1.96 * `Emp SD`,
    naive_lower = mean - 1.96 * `Avg SD`,  naive_upper = mean + 1.96 * `Avg SD`,
    stack_lower = mean - 1.96 * se_stack,  stack_upper = mean + 1.96 * se_stack
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(emp_lower, emp_upper, naive_lower, naive_upper,
             stack_lower, stack_upper),
    names_to = c("ci_type", ".value"), names_pattern = "(.*)_(lower|upper)"
  ) %>%
  mutate(ci_type = recode(ci_type,
                          "emp" = "Empirical SD", "naive" = "Simplified SD",
                          "stack" = "Stacked SD"))

# ── Combine — no NAs, each method only carries its own CI types ──
ci_all <- bind_rows(ci_orig, ci_oracle, ci_svy) %>%
  mutate(
    scenario = factor(scenario, levels = 6:1),
    method   = factor(method, levels = c("Original_RAILS", "Oracle_RAILS", "Cal_1")),
    ci_type  = factor(ci_type, levels = c("Empirical SD", "Simplified SD", "Stacked SD"))
  )

ci_colors <- c(
  "Empirical SD"  = "#E41A1C",
  "Simplified SD" = "#377EB8",
  "Stacked SD"    = "#4DAF4A"
)

# ── Plot: dodged point + bar per CI type ──
p_ci <- ggplot(ci_all, aes(x = scenario, y = mean, color = ci_type, group = ci_type)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
                width = 0.3, linewidth = 0.7,
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = true_value, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  facet_grid(method ~ ., scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = size_labels_fmt) +
  scale_color_manual(values = ci_colors, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(x = "Sample Size", y = "Mean Estimate", color = "CI Type",
       title = "95% Confidence Intervals by Method and SD Type") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        strip.text.y    = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"))

ggplotly(p_ci)
```
